titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mary de Rachewiltz Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 10, 2025 at 23:10

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, personal and professional papers, printed material, and sound recordings by or relating to Mary de Rachewiltz, former curator of the archive at the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries, and her father Ezra Pound.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 22 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1930s–2003. II. Writings, 1973–1986. III. Personal and Professional Papers, circa 1893–1987. IV. Printed Material, 1907–1990. V. Sound Recordings, 1974.

bioghist.head: Mary de Rachewiltz (1925-)
bioghist.p: Mary de Rachewiltz is an Italian American writer and translator, as well as former curator of the Ezra Pound archive at the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries of Yale University's Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library. She is the daughter of poet Ezra Pound and concert violinist Olga Rudge. Born Mary Rudge on July 9, 1925 in Brixen, Italy, she was raised by foster parents Jakob and Johanna Marcher in Gais in the province of South Tyrol. In later years, Rachewiltz lived with her birth parents at their home in Venice, where Pound worked with her on Italian translations of his poetry.

Rachewiltz wrote the autobiography Discretions (1971), later republished as Ezra Pound, Father and Teacher: Discretions in 2005. She also edited Ezra Pound to His Parents: Letters 1895-1929 (2011) with A. David Moody and Joanna Moody. She is the author of several books of Italian and English-language poetry, including Process in Verso (1973), Polittico (1996), and For the Wrong Reason (2002). Rachewiltz has published eight Italian translations of Pound's work, such as Selected Cantos (as Cantos Scelti , 1973) and the complete The Cantos (as I Cantos , 1985), and Pound's English translation of Confucius' Analects (as Analecta , 1995). She has also translated the poetry of Marianne Moore, e.e. cummings, and Denise Levertov. Rachewiltz was a fellow at Harvard University's Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study from 1973 to 1975 and a Canady Fellow at the University of Toledo in 1987.

In 1946, Rachewiltz married Egyptologist Boris de Rachewiltz, with whom she had two children, son Siegfried and daughter Patrizia. In the early 1950s, the Rachewiltz family moved to Brunnenburg Castle in South Tyrol. Rachewiltz founded the Ezra Pound Center for Literature at Brunnenburg, which hosts poetry workshops sponsored by the University of New Orleans.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Mary de Rachewiltz Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, personal and professional papers, printed material, and sound recordings by or relating to Mary de Rachewiltz, former curator of the archive at the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries, and her father Ezra Pound. Correspondence consists of letters exchanged by Pound and Rachewiltz, letters from her husband Boris de Rachewiltz and children Siegfried and Patrizia de Rachewiltz to Pound, and Rachewiltz's letters to Dorothy Pound. Also present is Rachewiltz's personal and professional correspondence with writers, editors, Pound scholars, and others in the United States and elsewhere. Correspondents include Mary Barnard, Leonard W. Doob, Valerie Eliot, Eva Hesse, Sanehide Kodama, James Laughlin and New Directions Publishing, Archibald MacLeish, Sheri Martinelli, Mary Bernetta Quinn, Olga Rudge, Vanni Scheiwiller, Carroll Franklin Terrell, and Harriet Zinnes. Writings include Rachewiltz's articles, talks, and translations, as well as broadsides and duplicates of Pound's Cantos. Personal and professional papers consist of personal documents, photographs, artwork of Pound by Henri Gaudier-Brzeska, and files pertaining to the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries and the Landwirtschaftliches Museum Brunnenburg in South Tyrol, as well as one of Pound's typewriters. Also present in the collection are works by other writers such as Duan Zhengyuan, Donald Gallup, Peter Russell, and William Butler Yeats.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Mary de Rachewiltz Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and his Contemporaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of family and professional correspondence of Ezra Pound and Mary de Rachewiltz. Included are letters exchanged by Pound and Rachewiltz; letters from Rachewiltz's husband and children to Pound; Rachewiltz's letters to Dorothy Pound; and a letter from Isabel W. Pound to a cousin.

The series also contains Rachewiltz's correspondence with writers, poets, editors, publishers, Pound scholars, colleagues, and other family and friends. Correspondents include writers Mary Barnard, Eva Hesse, Sanehide Kodama, James Laughlin, Archibald MacLeish, Sheri Martinelli, Mary Bernetta Quinn, Carroll Franklin Terrell, and Harriet Zinnes; Yale professor Leonard W. Doob; editor Valerie Eliot; publishers James Laughlin of New Directions and Vanni Scheiwiller; and Rachewiltz's mother Olga Rudge. A small amount of letters contain writings enclosed by the correspondents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Ezra Pound Correspondence and General Correspondence. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Pound, Ezra to Mary de Rachewiltz
Pound, Isabel W. to [first name unknown] Parker Monroe
Rachewiltz, Boris de to Ezra Pound
Rachewiltz, Mary de to Ezra Pound
Rachewiltz, Mary de to Dorothy Pound
Arts & Letters Center for Classical Studies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of drafts, notes, transcriptions, and proofs related to works by Mary de Rachewiltz and Ezra Pound. The series also contains writings by others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Writings of Mary de Rachewiltz, Writings of Ezra Pound, and Writings of Others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts, notes, and research material for Rachewiltz's articles, talks, and translations of Pound. There are also letter transcriptions for Ezra Pound to His Parents: Letters 1895–1929 , edited by Rachewiltz and others.

did.unittitle: Ezra Pound to His Parents: Letters 1895–1929 , transcriptions
Ezra Pound to His Parents: Letters 1895–1929 , transcriptions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains broadsides of The Cantos and a photocopy version of the source book for "Canto XLIX."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of drafts and proofs of other writers' scholarly works on Ezra Pound, as well as prose and poems. Included are works by Mary Barnard, Donald Gallup, Eva Hesse, James Laughlin, and Harriet Zinnes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Rachewiltz's personal documents, legal and estate files, event and exhibition files, material related to the Ezra Pound archive at the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries, and photographs of Pound and the Rachewiltz family. In addition to legal and medical documents related to Pound, the series contains artwork depicting Pound by Henri Gaudier-Brzeska and David Levine and a typewriter belonging to Pound. Also present are papers, printed ephemera, and offprints of Rachewiltz's mother Olga Rudge.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Artwork of Ezra Pound by others
Giovanni Giovannini's list of Ezra Pound books
Inventory of Ezra Pound poetry books
Landwirtschaftliches Museum Brunnenburg and South Tyrol
Military and Department of Justice documents related to Ezra Pound (photocopy)
Fenollosa, Ernest [captioned by Ezra Pound on verso]
Pound, Ezra, with Mary de Rachewiltz
Pound, Ezra, with Siegfried de Rachewiltz
Photographs and negatives for Ezra Pound, Father and Teacher: Discretions
Saint Elizabeths Hospital documents related to Ezra Pound (photocopy)
The Theatre Guild American Repertory Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed Material consists of books; clippings; English, French, and Italian dictionaries; event posters; exhibition catalogs and pamphlets; offprints and reprints; printed ephemera; scores; serials; and other printed matter. Most publications in this series are related to Ezra Pound, and many were donated to the archive of the Center for the Study of Ezra Pound and His Contemporaries by their authors or publishers. Rachewiltz's writings appears in few works.

Among the books belonging to Pound in this series are four volumes by William Butler Yeats, including A Packet for Ezra Pound , which was inscribed by Yeats. Also present is Pound's annotated copy of The Great Teacher's Lectures , a pamphlet featuring translated lectures by Duan Zhengyuan published by the World Peace Prayer Conference for the Exposition of Confucian Cosmopolitanism. Books written by Pound in this series were published during his lifetime and posthumously in English and Italian, including The Classic Noh Theatre of Japan with Ernest Fenollosa, Guide to Kulchur , The Spirit of Romance , and Women of Trachis , as well as collected poems, prose, and essays.

Books by others are in English, Chinese, Italian, and German and many were published by All'insegna del pesce d'oro, Libri Scheiwiller, and New Directions Publishing. Works by Mary de Rachewiltz include the poetry book Il Diapason and two versions of the autobiography Discretions . Other writers and editors present include Leonard W. Doob, Donald Gallup, Eva Hesse, Peter Russell, Carroll Franklin Terrell, and Harriet Zinnes.

Offprints and reprints were published in English, Italian and Japanese by Mary de Rachewiltz, Sanehide Kodama, Jacob Korg, Alan Levy, Toshikazu Niikura, Jean-Michel Rabaté, Caterina Ricciardi, G. Singh, and Emily Mitchell Wallace.

Serials consist of periodicals, journals, magazines, and newsletters in English, German, Italian, and Japanese. This series includes issues of Paideuma: A Journal Devoted to Ezra Pound Scholarship from 1972 to 1981, and The Analyst newsletter, which reprinted "A Guide to Ezra Pound's Cantos" by E. M. Glenn. Other serials include Agenda , Antaeus , Catai , Distel , Doshisha American Studies , Galleria , The Iowa Review , The Malahat Review , The Missouri Review , La nuova rivista europea , Poetry Australia , Quaderni di Tradizione mediterranea , St. Andrews Review , Sequoia , Stony Brook , Il verri , and The Yale Review .

did.unittitle: Duan, Zhengyuan. The Great Teacher's Lectures
Yeats, W. B. (William Butler). The Hour Glass
Yeats, W. B. (William Butler). A Packet for Ezra Pound

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Inscribed to Ezra Pound from W. B. Yeats, August 1929.

did.unittitle: Yeats, W. B. (William Butler). Poems Written in Discouragement, 1912–1913
Yeats, W. B. (William Butler). The Shadowy Waters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audio recordings of Rachewiltz reading The Cantos .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 22 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
